title: Delicate Peruvian Chicken Aji De Gallina
description: A comforting Peruvian dish featuring wood-fired baked chicken in a creamy mature cheddar sauce, this recipe is a floral and aerated twist on a classic, with a focus on low-calorie ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the chicken breasts with salt and black pepper.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and sear the chicken breasts until browned on both sides.
Transfer the chicken to a baking dish and bake in the wood-fired oven for 20 minutes, or until cooked through.
In a blender or food processor, puree the aji amarillo peppers, garlic, and walnuts until smooth.
In a saucepan, combine the heavy cream and pureed pepper mixture, and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and stir in the shredded mature cheddar cheese until melted and smooth.
To serve, slice the cooked chicken breasts and place on a plate, then spoon the creamy cheese sauce over the top and garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
